title: Capital Structure: If a firm has a high debt-to-equity ratio, it is...
summary: Assess how a firm's existing leverage level influences its acquisition financing choices.
prompt: If a firm has a high debt-to-equity ratio, it is more likely to finance acquisitions with equity.
solution: A high debt-to-equity ratio indicates that a firm is already highly leveraged, making it less likely to use equity for financing further acquisitions.
